source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/11_Clinical Evaluation/Clinical Image Report/002_Clinical Image Report - G1717FW.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for G1717FW against A1717MCW using plain radiographic images and DICOM 3.0 acquisition/output data.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical performance evaluation in technical documentation by comparing diagnostic radiographic image quality between the subject device G1717FW and comparative equipment A1717MCW.
keyPoints: Objective device is G1717FW by H&abyz; comparative equipment is A1717MCW made by H&Abyz.
The document lists X-ray conditions for 30 anatomical views and compares image findings, conclusions, clinical point of view, acquisition format, and exposure condition.
Most evaluated views report G1717FW and A1717MCW grades at or near S/Perfect, with total average shown as 3.93 for both devices.
The conclusion states G1717FW has a little better image quality and seems to be of more diagnostic value.
Clinical evaluation was conducted by Dr. Wonho Chang, described as a board-certified radiologist.

evidence.claim: The total average image evaluation value is reported as 3.93 for A1717MCW and 3.93 for G1717FW.
The grading scale defines S as Perfect=4, A as excellent=3, B as average=2, and C as poor=1.

evidence.claim: The conclusion states that G1717FW has a little better image quality and seems to have more diagnostic value.
evidence.evidenceText: Despite the image with slight difference, G1717FW has a little better image quality and the difference between two substances. In conclusion, G1717FW seems to be more of a diagnostic value.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the source text; CE/MDR appears only as source metadata authoritySignal and was not used as a document fact.
Conclusion text contains an apparent model inconsistency: it says images were taken with A1717MCW and F1717FW, while the document title, model, and objective device identify G1717FW.
Some summary averages conflict with earlier per-view evaluation pages, such as Skull Lat, Rib AP, 2nd Finger Lat, Forearm AP, Humerus AP, and Foot Lat.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including 'DCIOM 3.0', 'Date v', misspellings, and malformed table rows.
